name: Nelly Furtado
description:
'''Nelly Kim Furtado''' {{post-nominals|post-noms=[[Order of Prince Henry|ComIH]]}} ({{IPAc-en|f|ər|ˈ|t|ɑː|d|oʊ}} {{respell|fər|TAH|doh}}; {{IPA|pt-PT|fuɾˈtaðu|lang}}; born December 2, 1978) is a Canadian singer and songwriter. Known for her versatility and genre experimentation, she has sold more than 45 million records worldwide, making her one of the best-selling Canadian musicians.

Furtado rose to prominence with her [[trip hop]]-influenced debut studio album, ''[[Whoa, Nelly!]]'' (2000), which was a critical and commercial success and spawned the top-10 [[Billboard Hot 100|''Billboard'' Hot 100]] singles "[[I'm Like a Bird]]" and "[[Turn Off the Light]]". The former track earned her a [[Grammy Award for Best Female Pop Vocal Performance]]. She followed it with the more introspective, [[Folk music|folk]]-oriented [[Folklore (Nelly Furtado album)|''Folklore'']] (2003), which drew on her Portuguese heritage and performed less successfully than her debut.

Her third studio album, the [[Eclecticism in music|eclectic]]-pop ''[[Loose (Nelly Furtado album)|Loose]]'' (2006), sold over 10 million copies worldwide, becoming her best-selling release and one of the [[List of best-selling albums of the 21st century|best-selling albums of the 21st century]]. Marked by a reinvention of her image and sound, the album produced the worldwide number-one singles "[[Promiscuous (song)|Promiscuous]]" (featuring [[Timbaland]]), "[[Maneater (Nelly Furtado song)|Maneater]]", "[[Say It Right]]", and "[[All Good Things (Come to an End)]]". During this period, she also featured alongside [[Justin Timberlake]] on Timbaland's "[[Give It to Me (Timbaland song)|Give It to Me]]" (2007) and recorded the European hit duet "[[Broken Strings (song)|Broken Strings]]" with [[James Morrison (singer)|James Morrison]] (2008).

Furtado later released ''[[Mi Plan]]'' (2009), a Spanish-language album, which won the [[Latin Grammy Award for Best Female Pop Vocal Album]], followed by ''[[The Spirit Indestructible]]'' (2012). After becoming independent, she released [[The Ride (Nelly Furtado album)|''The Ride'']] (2017) through her own label, Nelstar Entertainment {{small|{{bracket|[[:pt:Nelstar|pt]]}}}}, and [[7 (Nelly Furtado album)|''7'']] (2024). Throughout her career, she has received numerous accolades, including one [[Grammy Awards|Grammy Award]] from seven nominations, one [[Latin Grammy Awards|Latin Grammy Award]], ten [[Juno Awards]], one [[Brit Awards|Brit Award]], one [[Billboard Music Awards|''Billboard'' Music Award]], one [[MTV Europe Music Awards|MTV Europe Music Award]], one [[World Music Awards|World Music Award]], and three [[MuchMusic Video Awards]]. She also has a star on [[Canada's Walk of Fame]] and was appointed a Commander of the [[Order of Prince Henry]] in 2014.
